artifact.note: The single replacement candidate is sealed and active for runtime use. Its classifier-aware five-case packaged focused set passed its non-certifying scope. The Campaign Debug canary and full-profile raw captures are mechanically complete but preliminary and unaccepted because the original wrapper missed timestamp-prefixed hard diagnostics; the full report is also red. The first candidate remains retained superseded evidence, and no result may mix their package identities.

foundation.summary: The retained source/resource contract gate passed inside the release-candidate build for its exact source HEAD.

workbench.summary: PC, PS4, PS5, XBOX_ONE, and XBOX_SERIES validation passed with one common CRC, zero first-party hard errors, and zero cleanup or monitored-boundary residue for the candidate source HEAD.

packagedFocusedAutotests.summary: All five individually named service suites passed against the exact staged packed candidate with 5/0/0/0 JUnit totals, valid exact diagnostic classification, zero unapproved hard diagnostics, 40 rehashed envelope files, and zero cleanup or spill residue. This does not certify any higher proof rung.

focusedForceAuthority.summary: State-only combat-presence, ownership-transition, and town-influence fixtures passed; native world, package, network, restart breadth, performance, and soak remain outside this result.

fullCampaignDebug.summary: The current full profile consumed the exact active packed candidate and produced stable, rehashed, zero-residue raw artifacts, but the original wrapper's reported success is not an acceptance result: its regex missed timestamp-prefixed errors. The corrected canonical-log audit found 25 hard diagnostics, including 13 exact proof-bound intentional fixtures and 10 unapproved diagnostics; the report also remained red at 584 PASS, 49 WARN, 46 FAIL, 7 BLOCKED, and 1 SKIPPED.

proofRungs.label: Static/source/resource contracts
proofRungs.summary: Retained Foundation evidence is green inside the immutable candidate build for its exact source HEAD.

proofRungs.label: Enforce compile and configuration
proofRungs.summary: All five explicit Workbench targets are retained and green for the candidate source and canonical four-file package build.

proofRungs.label: Deterministic service contracts
proofRungs.summary: All five release-critical service suites passed individually against the exact staged packed candidate with 5/0/0/0 JUnit totals, exact classified diagnostics, and zero residue. This scoped rung does not inherit into native-world or later gates.

proofRungs.label: Native engine-world behavior
proofRungs.summary: The current package-bound Campaign Debug capture is preliminary and red: its full report contains native mission-runtime and physicalization failures, and its corrected diagnostic census contains unapproved hard errors. Scoped passing cuts remain useful, but this rung cannot be partial-green while a current subset fails.

proofRungs.label: Packaged dedicated server
proofRungs.summary: The active replacement candidate is sealed, but it has not yet been launched through the standard dedicated-server runtime gate.

proofRungs.label: Multiple clients, reconnect, and JIP
proofRungs.summary: Host, two-client, reconnect, late-join, and packet-disruption convergence are not certified.

proofRungs.label: Fresh-process restart and fault injection
proofRungs.summary: Selected journal, shutdown, field-vehicle, exact-QRF, counterattack, and rebuild cuts pass; the arbitrary full campaign graph and fault matrix remain open.

proofRungs.label: Performance and long soak
proofRungs.summary: The reported one-second hitch and long-campaign capacity limits have no current immutable-package soak evidence.

proofRungs.label: Canary release
proofRungs.summary: Blocked by every lower unproven release rung.

proofRungs.label: Stable certification
proofRungs.summary: No current matrix row is certified.

activeBlockers.summary: The replacement candidate is sealed and runtime-eligible with exact package and tool identities, and its packaged focused service rung passed; Campaign Debug must be rerun through the corrected fail-closed diagnostic classifier, while the current full report is red and dedicated, multiplayer/JIP, restart breadth, performance, and soak remain pending.

Current package-bound Campaign Debug produced a mechanically complete raw capture but not accepted evidence: the corrected audit found 1 unapproved canary and 10 unapproved full-run hard diagnostics. The full report also has 46 failed cases, 7 blocked cases, 112 failed required assertions, and 14 blocked required assertions.
Known command-menu and modal-map defects remain open until source correction plus rendered packaged-client proof.
Natural sustained infantry and convoy travel, identical-waypoint suppression, and measured no-stutter behavior are not proven.
Campaign read-model convergence is not proven with host, two clients, reconnect, JIP, restart, and marker-cap boundaries.
The one-second hitch and bounded-history admission cliffs remain unclosed.
Configured mission breadth still exceeds mission-specific CE 3.11.1 behavioral parity and proof.
Development proof and destructive diagnostic surfaces have not yet been separated from the release package.
